title: GENE REFERENCE
intro_html: Each animal carries a set of genes inherited from its parent at birth. On reproduction, a random number of genes mutate multiplicatively: gene_new = gene_old × (1 + N(0, mutation_strength)). Probability-like genes are clamped to [0, 1].

genes.name: eat_energy
genes.description_html: Energy gained per successful meal. Prey gains it from grass on its cell; predators gain it from catching prey. Final energy is capped at max_energy.

genes.name: move_cost
genes.description_html: Energy spent per cell moved. Total cost per step equals move_cost × cells_moved (see move_distance).

genes.name: max_energy
genes.description_html: Upper cap on stored energy. Eating never pushes energy above this value.

genes.name: reproduce_energy
genes.description_html: Energy threshold for reproduction. The animal must have at least this much energy to attempt reproducing.

genes.name: reproduce_chance
genes.description_html: Per-step probability of attempting reproduction once above reproduce_energy. Clamped to [0, 1].

genes.name: escape_chance
genes.description_html: Probability the prey escapes a predator attack on the same cell. The encounter outcome is: P(catch) = hunt_success × (1 - escape_chance). Clamped to [0, 1].

genes.name: hunt_success
genes.description_html: Predator's catch effectiveness when on the same cell as prey. See escape_chance above. Clamped to [0, 1].

genes.name: move_distance
genes.description_html: Cells the animal moves per step. Stored as a float - the integer part is guaranteed cells, the fractional part is the probability of one extra cell (e.g. 1.7 = 1 cell certain + 70% chance of a 2nd). Energy cost (via move_cost) scales linearly with actual cells moved.

genes.name: max_age
genes.description_html: Age at which senescence (natural death from old age) kicks in. Past max_age, per-step death probability grows linearly: P_death = 0.05 × (age / max_age - 1), capped at 1.0. Most individuals die naturally around 1.3-1.5 × max_age. Below max_age there is no aging mortality.

genes.name: metabolism
genes.description_html: Passive energy drain subtracted every step regardless of activity. Lower metabolism = survives longer between meals; higher = burns out faster but supports more activity.

genes.name: offspring_investment
genes.description_html: Fraction of the parent's current energy transferred to its offspring at birth. Energy is conserved - what the parent loses, the child gains as starting energy. High investment = strong but rare offspring (K strategy); low = many weak offspring (r strategy). Clamped to [0, 1].

mutation_intro_html: Three global parameters in the Mutation section control how genes mutate at reproduction:

mutation_params.name: mutation_min
mutation_params.description_html: Minimum number of genes that mutate per offspring.

mutation_params.name: mutation_max
mutation_params.description_html: Maximum number of genes that mutate per offspring. Per child, a random integer in [mutation_min, mutation_max] genes are randomly selected and mutated.

mutation_params.name: mutation_strength
mutation_params.description_html: Standard deviation σ of the multiplicative gaussian noise applied to each mutated gene. mutation_strength = 0.1 means a typical mutation changes the gene by about ±10%.
